articles.description:
Crumbl introduces the new Andes Green Mint Brownie as part of this week's limited-time rotation. With St. Patrick's Day coming up this week, the lineup features green and rainbows.

Here's the weekly rotation of desserts at Crumbl from March 16 through March 21, 2026:
- New Andes Green Mint Brownie - A chocolate brownie embedded with Andes Creme De Menthe pieces, topped with green buttercream, chocolate, and more Andes pieces.
- Mallow Creme Cookie featuring Lucky Charms - A warm graham cracker and white drop-packed cookie finished with fluffy vanilla marshmallow frosting and magical Lucky Charms™ marshmallows.
- Key Lime Pie Cookie - A chilled graham cookie topped with a zesty lime filling, a rosette of whipped cream, graham cracker crumbs, and a fresh lime wedge.
- Cereal Milk Cookie - A warm, cereal-inspired sugar cookie brushed with a milky glaze and topped with colorful, fruity cereal.
Oatmeal Cookie Thins will be available for this week's Thin Thursday.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Bakes New Andes Green Mint Brownie

articles.description:
Shake Shack's K-Shack BBQ Burger features a quarter-pound Angus beef patty (or two or three), American cheese, crispy-fried sweet onions, fresh scallions, and Korean BBQ sauce, served on a toasted potato bun.

I went with a double-patty version, which costs $14.29, but made use of the current BOGO deal.
Oddly enough, the Double K-Shack BBQ Burger brought to mind McDonald's McArch, which I tried (and reviewed) recently. Both burgers sport a half-pound of beef and a strong onion presence (both feature crispy onions, but the McArch has slivered white onions while the K-Shack contains scallions/green onions).



Perhaps getting the double patty was the wrong way to go, as the Korean BBQ sauce came in as a light touch here. The single-patty version might have been a better showcase for it. If y…
articles.title: Review: Shake Shack - K-Shack BBQ Burger

articles.description:
Shake Shack is offering a limited-time, mix-and-match deal through Monday, March 16, 2026, where if you buy any item from its Korean-Style Menu, you can get a second one free when ordering digitally.


The promotion is good on all Korean-Style menu items, including:


K-Shack Fried Chicken Sandwich
K-Shack BBQ Burger
K-Shack Spicy BBQ Fries
K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites
K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake


To redeem the deal, you need to add any two Korean-Style menu items to an order placed via the Shake Shack website or mobile app, or an in-restaurant kiosk using the code "SPICYSWEET". As is typical of such deals, the cheaper item is free.
The BOGO can be used in conjunction with Shake Shack's digital-exclusive $1 sodas, $3 crinkle-cut fries, and $5 classic shakes deals.

articles.description:
Panda Express has partnered with spicy ramen brand Buldak to launch new Dynamite Sweet & Sour Chicken as the chain's "spiciest dish to date."

The new dish features crispy-fried chicken bites wok-tossed with red bell peppers and onions in a "vibrant, tangy sauce amped up with Buldak's signature fiery heat." Panda Express describes Dynamite Sweet & Sour Chicken's taste as a "'swicy' (sweet and spicy) profile hits with a balanced tanginess upfront, followed by an intense, lingering burn." Each order includes a free Buldak Original Hot Sauce Stick (while supplies last) to add heat as you like.
The new entree was tested in select cities in August 2025, and the national run is available now through May 25, 2026, while supplies last.
Dynamite Sweet & Sour Chicken is priced in line with the chain's regular entrees (i.e., no premium upcharge). Prices vary by location.
Photo via Panda Express.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Panda Express Launches New Dynamite Sweet & Sour Chicken

articles.description:
Krispy Kreme has rolled out its 2026 St. Patrick's Day donuts to participating locations across the US. The donut chain is also offering a deal where you can get an Original Glazed dozen for 13 cents with the purchase of any dozen donuts at regular price on Friday, March 13, 2026.

This year's St. Patrick's Day Collection is now available through Tuesday, March 17, 2026, and includes:
- Shooting Shamrock Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in green vanilla-flavored icing, topped with light green icing loops and a shamrock sugar piece.    
- Over the Rainbow Donut - A shell donut filled with Original Kreme, dipped in light green vanilla-flavored icing, topped with shamrock rainbow sprinkles and a rainbow sugar piece.    
- Plaid Party Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in vanilla-flavored icing, topped with green icing drizzles.  
- Chocolate Iced with St. Patrick’s Sprinkles Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in chocolate icing, topped with St. Patrick's Day sprinkles.    
The 13-cent Original Glazed dozen deal at participating locations is available in-person and online for pickup and delivery through the Krispy Kreme app and website using the promo code "13". It's limited to one use per customer.
Photo via Krispy Kreme.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Krispy Kreme's 2026 St. Patrick's Day Donuts Arrive

articles.description:
The flavors of New Orleans are back at Applebee's with the return of the limited-time Big Easy Menu, with prices starting at $11.99.

Applebee's Big Easy Menu includes:
- New Bayou Shrimp Bowl (pictured) - Blackened shrimp, Andouille sausage, Spanish rice, garlicky green beans, and sauteed bell peppers & onions with a side of creamy Cajun sauce.
- New Big Easy Burger - A beef patty with pepper jack cheese, grilled Andouille sausage, zesty citrus slaw, and spicy honey mustard on a brioche bun, served with classic fries.
- Bourbon Street Chicken & Andouille Sausage Penne (pictured) - Bourbon Street chicken, seared Andouille sausage slices, seasoned bell peppers, and onion in a Cajun Alfredo sauce over penne pasta.
The menu also includes regular menu items such as the Bourbon Street Chicken & Shrimp Skillet, Bourbon Street Steak, and the Bourbon Street Mushroom Swiss Burger.
In addition to the Big Easy Menu, Applebee's readies for St. Patrick's Day with a series of limited-time drinks, including: 
- New $6 Emerald Escape - Jameson Irish Whiskey, Malibu Coconut Rum, melon liqueur, blue raspberry, pineapple juice, and lime served in a signature Mucho glass garnished with a gold sugar rim.
- New $6 Pricklycane - A fruity twist on a NOLA hurricane featuring Bacardi Superior Rum, premium lemon sour, prickly pear, orange, and pineapple juices. Like the Emerald Escape, it is served in a signature Mucho glass garnished with a gold sugar rim.
- New $4 Parade Punch Mocktail - A blend of pineapple, prickly pear, blue raspberry, premium lemon sour, and lemon lime soda, garnished with a gold sugar-dusted lemon wedge.
Photo via Applebee's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Applebee's Big Easy Menu Returns

articles.description:
Baskin-Robbins launches new Banana Dulce de Leche ice cream as its March 2026 Flavor of the Month. Additionally, the ice cream parlor chain offers suitably green desserts for St. Patrick's Day, a new Custom Cake in a Box, and a $6 Banana Split Wednesday deal for rewards members.

Baskin-Robbins' Banana Dulce de Leche ice cream is inspired by banoffee pie (a portmanteau of "banana" and "toffee"). The limited-time flavor features frozen banana custard and brown butter ice cream with toffee pieces and dulce de leche swirls.
For St. Patrick's Day, the chain has a seasonal St. Patrick's Day ice cream cake as well as its regular-menu Mint Chocolate Chip Milkshake and Pre-Packed Quarts. Through the end of the month, Baskin-Robbins Rewards members can redeem a deal for $5 off a cake of $35 or more.
Baskin-Robbins' new Custom Cake in a Box features your choice of cake, ice cream flavor, and toppings. It's a build-your-own option for the previously available Cake in a Box.
The $6 Banana Split Wednesday deal is available every Wednesday in March for Baskin-Robbins Rewards members at participating locations nationwide. Prices vary, but a Banana Split normally costs $9.29 at my local Baskin-Robbins location here in Los Angeles.
Photo via Baskin-Robbins.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Baskin-Robbins Churns Out New Banana Dulce de Leche Ice Cream and More

articles.description:
Checkers & Rally's welcome new flatbread combo meals, featuring new Flatbread Sandwiches, with prices ranging from $5 to $6.

At the $5 mark, the Spicy Chicken Flatbread Combo includes a new Spicy Chicken Flatbread Sandwich, small Famous Seasoned Fries, and a small drink. The new sandwich features two halves of a spicy chicken patty, iceberg lettuce, tomato, and ranch, served in a grilled and folded flatbread.
Starting at $6, the Bacon Burger Flatbread Combo includes the new Bacon Burger Flatbread Sandwich, small Famous Seasoned Fries, and a small drink. The Bacon Burger Flatbread Sandwich features two halves of a beef patty, American cheese, a strip of bacon, dill pickles, crispy-fried onions, lettuce, and Sweet 'N Smoky BBQ Sauce, served in a folded, grilled flatbread.
Both Flatbread Sandwiches can also be purchased alone. Prices may vary, but the Spicy Chicken Flatbread Sandwich costs $3.99 near me in Los Angeles, while the Bacon Burger Flatbread Sandwich costs $4.99.
Checkers & Rally's is also offering a limited-time deal where you can get two Big Bufords for $10 at participating locations. The Big Buford is the chains' signature premium burger and features two beef patties, two slices of American cheese, iceberg lettuce, tomato, red onion, dill pickles, ketchup, mustard, and mayo, on a toasted bakery-style bun. A Big Buford normally costs $6.99 near me.
Photo via Checkers & Rally's.
